textByHashes.21765fc05e6328d2d7295308418762fa07ff30de: Hi there. I'm Ektheme, an expert on the Xerician era.
textByHashes.028a530eed72f15a91a0566155eced2dc5f15c49: Xeric was a dark mage who ruled Great Kourend for at least ten decades. A time period that is sometimes known as the Age of Strife.
textByHashes.b74b18272f26ed2a871ca5286e2ffecaa16d9cda: We do have records of course. However, while Xeric was indeed ruler of the kingdom from the 37th to the 47th decades, some believe that he actually ruled for longer.
textByHashes.80a11ca5ef43c4bec5cdd334fad1ec5426ee14ca: It is often speculated that Xeric and his predecessor, King Shayzien VII, were in fact the same person.
textByHashes.edf40498b8085bfd966ed2015837ae91d3e8d90a: Like Xeric, King Shayzien VII was fascinated with the Dark Altar.
textByHashes.1934e65e428f0ac5836fdba5f27c0eba97aa5c20: The King was killed at the Battle of the Concourse during the 37th decade. We have many accounts of this, yet his body was never recovered.
textByHashes.e82625d8139cdc340a8f687fe45a514bc6c67266: Just days after his death, we have the first recorded mention of Xeric, an incredibly powerful mage who resembled the King.
textByHashes.79b25e62d24fbd5a9ce6f085b02acfe6d0ecc405: It is written that Xeric killed the rulers of Kourend's five cities and claimed the kingdom as his own.
textByHashes.d7f8cc2ba50bec1aaedf38306c54c027bd8cb502: It's possible. I am no expert on necromancy but we know the Dark Altar has the power to resurrect beasts. We also know that it can extend life. It's how the people of Arceuus are immortal.
textByHashes.930372fed97ca33004905bcd74675917dc939075: King Shayzien VII may have found a way to combine these powers.
textByHashes.ec96d8f6a5a62fe11db92963cc4dd1057661a974: Xeric drew much of his power from the Dark Altar, but he asserted his authority as ruler through his personal army of creatures, the lizardmen.
textByHashes.19072a11641ba19b1c4d65e82d5cf3db77b52b9b: Sadly so, though we're not exactly sure how he did so. Xeric used the citizens of Kourend for many experiments, as well as for his own sadistic pleasures.
textByHashes.ba17ab062240278fbae31eeb1699d967c82f2412: We have little written record of the experiments, but we believe they might have led to the creation of the lizardmen.
textByHashes.51d43a97ffe3596508971460f52dc9b33e325b44: Originally, we believed that Xeric created the lizardmen. However, recent discovieres suggest they were actually created during the time of King Shayzien VII.
textByHashes.6b4267bf4ad967915d43292708a148a7d6f047d9: Xeric was undoubtedly powerful, but he couldn't rule the people singlehandedly, nor could he rely on human informants. He needed an army he could control.
textByHashes.b5f8229b71e58bf5bde0b73ba924c91c5c0ec91c: Lizardmen are loyal creatures and would do as he commanded without question. With them at his disposal, the people were powerless to resist his authority.
textByHashes.7e3f7a0b9986d10649bae3421336fe8635d47260: They remain loyal to Xeric. Even to this day they continue to attack the kingdom, as you may have seen.
textByHashes.e5664729082062988a0945d976a6aa17c0f8a799: Fortunately, the Shayzien Army keeps them under control.
textByHashes.3a197bc886d33af85064c76836ef613bdf7bfbac: Without examining their minds, we will never know for sure. However, we assume Xeric genetically altered them to enforce that their duty is to serve him.
textByHashes.5dface59a80bb52bb8f324f8303c1aad6e8c6b43: A good question. One that I'm afraid I do not know the answer to.
textByHashes.f7825bc280cfc4da41b81005709c51930aabd952: Is there anything else you would like to know?
textByHashes.9793438c856bf8c0c5a1701ebb076851b1570ce8: During the 47th decade, after ten decades of oppression, the people revolted against Xeric and his army of lizardmen.
textByHashes.d69312dd9288cde46866ba452c8da3da65006a49: The events were triggered through the courage of one man, Byrne. He was not a man of particular stature of nobility, though he did proceed to become king.
textByHashes.8cd7229f881b1aa1f7f4e4a7398875e224695fac: 'Twas the 312th day of decade 47. Byrne, a farmer by trade, is said to have witnessed a lizardman guard murder a child whilst working his farm.
textByHashes.404ba89ed5490a30735241739010513e737f626b: Outraged at what he had just seen, Byrne grabbed his pitchfork and sprinted towards the guard.
textByHashes.09dc25df6bf71bd715766f1109b5be8ad11fbfea: Blinded by fury, Byrne attacked the guard, slaughtering the lizardman in plain sight of all around.
textByHashes.e67e821b56e554dd5311185a0fbee775a5a9da67: Legend has it Byrne then gave a galvanizing speech, standing over the butchered guard with his bloodied pitchfork held aloft in the air, urging the people to fight back.
textByHashes.2f0c139c0ba5beb3ea67306fecf48c7832fb26a4: Sadly no. While we have many witness accounts, they all differ, so we can not verify it at all.
textByHashes.7f67ecdf8f765f7b4bb733fa703e86634c07be0b: Whatever Byrne said, the people responded. Together, the citizens of Kourend revolted, grabbing whatever they could use as a weapon. They fought the lizardmen.
textByHashes.54a706af4d142356386885db5e17ebbdf4b27883: The lizardmen, while strong and powerful, were simply outnumbered. In a matter of days, they had been driven back into the Kebos Lowlands.
textByHashes.a0eeb5dea3401bd9823437c354196f75e37ae7b1: During the battle, the temple surrounding the Dark Altar was destroyed, weakening Xeric's power. We believe he fled to Quidamortem.
textByHashes.548ca590a53c6a5bc00dc6f1c8d6c26f3bd3fd5f: Some say Xeric was actually killed during the revolt, but we have no record of his remains being discovered.
textByHashes.73ef6c9e055b2507efe0a2bff9020c542bf9fd4d: Xeric was incredibly powerful but we can reasonably assume that he soon perished. Without the power of the Dark Altar, Xeric would have become mortal.
textByHashes.9f72c2a158ad981da87c2264da99495f64c53a28: Indeed. Is there anything else I can help you with?
textByHashes.cc0d62bbd58561f5f40d2bee0c3ed618dba1d94b: Xeric was incredibly powerful, but without the Dark Altar, he would have soon become mortal and perished.
textByHashes.65e78af1723e543ae2ec9b9b8c6b80776c43cf23: However, recent discoveries suggest that he may have found a way to amplify the power of the altar. This may have allowed him to live far longer than previously believed.
textByHashes.dd241fadb13f9f3ccf23fb54523268ea556e18a5: Is there anything else I can help you with?
